title: Tamil Chicken 65

ingredients.name: boneless chicken thighs, cut into 3 cm pieces

subs.name: cauliflower florets (200 g) for gobi 65 — fry for only 4 minutes
2 tbsp lemon juice for a dairy-free version
rice flour for a crispier, gluten-friendly crust
omit — add 1 extra tbsp cornflour to bind the batter

ingredients.name: red chilli powder (Kashmiri for colour, or regular)

steps.text: In a large bowl, mix curd, cornflour, egg white, ginger-garlic paste, red chilli powder, turmeric, black pepper and salt into a smooth, thick batter. Add the chicken pieces and coat thoroughly — the batter should cling to each piece. Marinate for 20 minutes.

Heat oil in a kadai to 175°C — test by dropping a small piece of batter; it should rise to the surface within 3 seconds. If it sinks and stays down, the oil is too cool; if it immediately darkens, it is too hot.
Lower the chicken pieces in batches of 8–10 — do not crowd. Fry on medium-high heat for 5–6 minutes until deep red and crispy. Drain on kitchen paper. Repeat with remaining batches.
Carefully drop the curry leaves and slit green chillies into the hot oil for 30 seconds until crisp, then scoop them out.

Toss the fried chicken with the crispy curry leaves and green chillies. Squeeze lemon juice over immediately and serve on a plate with sliced onion rings and a wedge of lemon. Chicken 65 waits for no one — serve within minutes of frying.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: curd → 2 tbsp lemon juice for a dairy-free version (batter will be thinner); egg → omit — add 1 extra tbsp cornflour to bind the batter; cornflour → rice flour for a crispier, gluten-friendly crust; chicken → cauliflower florets (200 g) for gobi 65 — fry for only 4 minutes
